what: ROS 2 Python package rclpy was not found when launching run_vln_m3pro.py
why: The controller was launched inside the conda base environment, which did not include the ROS 2 Humble Python site packages.
how_fixed: I deactivated conda and launched with /usr/bin/python3 after sourcing /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash.
commands: conda deactivate 2>/dev/null || true; source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash; /usr/bin/python3 scripts/real_robot/run_vln_m3pro.py --backbone auto --safety-radius 0.50 --trace-dir results/vln_runs --cert-dir results/certificates
validation: The banner changed from ROS2: NOT FOUND to ROS2: available, and the FleetSafe-VLN node reported ready.

what: git pull, make help, and run_vln_m3pro.py failed on the Jetson.
why: The Jetson directory existed but was not a real git clone and did not contain the new scripts.
how_fixed: I treated the RTX desktop as the controller host and the Jetson as the sensing/action host, then documented rsync as the later Jetson synchronization method.
commands: rsync -av --exclude data/real_robot_bags --exclude logs ~/robotics/FleetSafe-VisualNav-Benchmark/ jetson@172.20.10.14:~/robotics/FleetSafe-VisualNav-Benchmark/
validation: The desktop could see /scan0, /scan1, /odom_raw, /camera/color/image_raw, and the /fleetsafe/* topics over ROS_DOMAIN_ID=30.

what: Publishing to /fleetsafe/instruction_text waited forever for a subscription.
why: The controller was either not running, running in another domain, or stopped by earlier errors.
how_fixed: I verified the subscription using ros2 topic info and then relaunched the controller in a clean terminal.
commands: ros2 topic info /fleetsafe/instruction_text -v
validation: Subscription count became 1 and the node name fleetsafe_vln_controller appeared.

what: make vln-robot and make vln-robot-live initially did not exist locally.
why: The local repository had not pulled the commit that added the VLN targets, and the help text did not expose all targets.
how_fixed: I pulled the repository, added a VLN section to make help, and verified the help output.
commands: git pull --ff-only origin main; make help | grep -A 35 "VLN"
validation: The help output listed vln-desktop, vln-desktop-radius, vln-send, watchers, and audits.

what: No /scan0, /scan1, /odom_raw, or /camera topics appeared at first.
why: The Yahboom stack and ROS 2 workspaces were not sourced in the active shell.
how_fixed: I sourced the Yahboom workspace and confirmed /YB_Node plus sensor topics.
commands: source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash; source ~/yahboomcar_ws/install/setup.bash; export ROS_DOMAIN_ID=30; ros2 node list; ros2 topic list -t | grep -Ei "cmd_vel|odom|imu|scan|camera|image|depth"
validation: /YB_Node, /cmd_vel, /odom_raw, /scan0, /scan1, and later /camera/color/image_raw appeared.

what: The CBF layer latched e-stop with reason cbf_infeasible.
why: The LiDAR reported obstacles or effective clearance below the configured safety radius. This was a correct safety behavior.
how_fixed: I moved the robot/obstacles when needed, lowered the dry-run radius only for debugging, and never enabled motion while below the safety radius.
commands: SAFETY_RADIUS=0.20 make vln-check-stack; make vln-desktop-radius RADIUS=0.20
validation: When effective clearance rose to about 0.33 m with radius 0.20 m, the certificate showed safe=true and qp_status=optimal.

what: The scans contained 0.0 m or 0.05 m readings that made the raw minimum look unsafe.
why: These were dead-zone or invalid beams near the scanner minimum range rather than usable obstacle clearance.
how_fixed: I added an audited LiDAR sanitizer that discards dead-zone artifacts and uses a conservative fifth-percentile effective clearance.
commands: make vln-lidar-inspect; SAFETY_RADIUS=0.20 make vln-check-stack
validation: The inspector printed raw_min, valid_min, p05, invalid count, combined effective clearance, and the CBF decision.

what: Some early runs produced empty certificate and trace JSONL files.
why: The controller returned early on e-stop or infeasible paths before writing evidence.
how_fixed: I changed the controller so every instruction goes through a single evidence-emission path before any return.
commands: LATEST_CERT="$(ls -td results/certificates/* | head -1)"; tail -n 1 "$LATEST_CERT"/vln_certificates_m3pro.jsonl | python3 -m json.tool
validation: Every instruction now creates a [VLN-EVIDENCE] console line plus non-empty JSONL certificate and trace records.

where: Makefile line around the VLN help section
what: make failed with missing separator.
why: A quoted echo line was broken across lines without a proper tab-prefixed Makefile recipe line.
how_fixed: I rewrote the affected help line so each @echo is complete and tab-indented.
commands: nl -ba Makefile | sed -n "726,740p"; make -n vln-lidar-inspect
validation: make help and make -n vln-lidar-inspect executed again.

what: The LiDAR inspector launched as /usr/bin//usr/bin/python3 and failed.
why: A replacement script accidentally replaced python3 twice.
how_fixed: I normalized interpreter calls to exactly /usr/bin/python3.
commands: grep -R "/usr/bin//usr/bin/python3" -n Makefile scripts/live || true; make -n vln-lidar-inspect
validation: The dry-run target printed /usr/bin/python3 scripts/live/inspect_lidar_clearance.py.

what: The camera topic existed but initially had no publisher or no live frame rate.
why: The Orbbec/Dabai camera launch was not active yet, and ROS topic tools on this ROS version did not accept the attempted --qos-profile argument.
how_fixed: I launched the camera stack on the Jetson and verified publisher count and frame rate using the available ros2 topic hz command.
commands: ros2 node list | grep -Ei "camera|orbbec"; ros2 topic info /camera/color/image_raw -v; timeout 10s ros2 topic hz /camera/color/image_raw
validation: The camera publisher appeared, the controller logged first camera frame received, and certificates showed camera_seen=true.

what: I needed to prove voice is a first-class input path, not only text.
why: A serious VLN robot must accept natural spoken instructions when the platform has a voice module.
how_fixed: I published a voice transcript to /fleetsafe/instruction_voice and recorded the resulting trace/certificate.
commands: ros2 topic pub --once /fleetsafe/instruction_voice std_msgs/msg/String "{data: 'move forward slowly while avoiding nearby obstacles'}"
validation: The certificate source field became voice, camera_seen=true, qp_status=optimal, and the trace included raw_instruction plus grounded avoid=obstacles.

what: Watchers reported that topics did not exist.
why: ROS 2 topics are not visible until the controller is running and, for some outputs, until an instruction has been processed.
how_fixed: I kept the controller terminal open and sent a test instruction before expecting latched-like data.
commands: make vln-desktop-radius RADIUS=0.20; make vln-send TEXT="move forward slowly"; make vln-watch-cert
validation: Parsed instruction, nominal Twist, and certificate JSON were printed by watcher targets.

what: The checker displayed radius 0.30 m while the controller was launched with radius 0.20 m.
why: The checker used a default rather than respecting the SAFETY_RADIUS environment variable.
how_fixed: I changed the target to pass SAFETY_RADIUS=${SAFETY_RADIUS:-default}.
commands: SAFETY_RADIUS=0.20 make vln-check-stack
validation: The health check header displayed Safety radius: 0.20 m.

what: Many raw run directories appeared as untracked files.
why: Each dry-run creates timestamped results. Most are ephemeral evidence, not all should be committed.
how_fixed: I selected one clean evidence package and committed only results/demo_evidence_voice_vln_real_robot.
commands: git add results/demo_evidence_voice_vln_real_robot/; git commit -m "evidence: add voice-conditioned real-robot VLN safety trace"; git push origin main
validation: Commit 8bef749 contains the voice certificate, trace, pretty JSON files, and README.

when: Evidence lookup after leaving the repository
what: The command `ls -td results/certificates/*` failed with no such file or directory.
why: I was in `~` rather than `~/robotics/FleetSafe-VisualNav-Benchmark`, so the relative results path was wrong.
how_fixed: I made `cd ~/robotics/FleetSafe-VisualNav-Benchmark` the first line of every evidence-inspection block.
commands:
cd ~/robotics/FleetSafe-VisualNav-Benchmark
LATEST_CERT="$(ls -td results/certificates/* | head -1)"
validation: The JSON certificate printed correctly after running from the repository root.

what: The controller reported `LiDAR stale (... > 1.0s). Emergency stop.`
why: The controller had not received fresh scan messages within the freshness timeout, either because the Jetson stack was not active or discovery was inconsistent.
how_fixed: I verified the Jetson scan rates and restarted the controller only after live scan data appeared.
commands:
timeout 5s ros2 topic hz /scan0
timeout 5s ros2 topic hz /scan1
validation: Both scans reported roughly 7 Hz, after which stale_lidar was no longer the blocker.

when: Safety radius conflict with physical scene
what: The robot stopped even at a 0.30 m safety radius.
why: The physical scene had objects around 0.28 m from the scanner, so the controller was correct to block motion.
how_fixed: I did not override the safety layer; I used radius 0.20 only for dry-run evidence in a tight space and kept live motion disabled.
commands: make vln-desktop-radius RADIUS=0.20
validation: The certificate changed from cbf_infeasible to safe=true only when effective clearance exceeded the active radius.

what: The parsed/certificate watchers sometimes missed messages.
why: The FleetSafe output topics are volatile ROS 2 topics, not durable latched topics. A watcher that starts after the message can miss it.
how_fixed: I documented that watchers should be started before sending the instruction, and that the JSONL files are the durable evidence source.
commands:
make vln-watch-cert
make vln-send TEXT="move forward slowly"
validation: The watcher displayed the certificate when active before or near the time of publication, and JSONL always preserved it.

what: `ros2 topic hz /camera/color/image_raw --qos-profile sensor_data` failed.
why: The installed ROS 2 CLI version did not support that argument for the command being used.
how_fixed: I used the supported command and verified publisher count with `ros2 topic info -v`.
commands:
ros2 topic info /camera/color/image_raw -v
timeout 10s ros2 topic hz /camera/color/image_raw
validation: Publisher count became 1 and frame rate printed around 1 Hz.

what: `make vln-desktop-radius` refused to launch because run_vln_m3pro.py was already running.
why: The wrapper intentionally prevents multiple controllers from competing for the same topics.
how_fixed: I killed stale controller processes before relaunching or kept the running terminal open.
commands: pkill -9 -f "run_vln_m3pro.py" 2>/dev/null || true
validation: A clean launch produced a fresh timestamped trace and certificate directory.

when: Dry-run decision looked like no motion
what: The decision field was `dry_run_zero` even when the safety filter produced a nonzero safe command.
why: Dry-run mode intentionally publishes zero to `/cmd_vel` while still recording the safe command that would have been sent in live mode.
how_fixed: I documented the distinction between `u_safe` and the actual dry-run output to prevent overclaiming.
commands: tail -n 1 "$LATEST_CERT"/vln_certificates_m3pro.jsonl | python3 -m json.tool
validation: The certificate showed u_safe=[0.040...,0.0], dry_run=true, decision=dry_run_zero.

when: Emergency stop latch after infeasible CBF
what: Once CBF infeasible occurred, later commands could be ignored or blocked.
why: A latched e-stop is safer than silently resuming after a hard safety violation.
how_fixed: I restarted the controller after moving the robot or changing the dry-run radius.
commands: pkill -9 -f "run_vln_m3pro.py"; make vln-desktop-radius RADIUS=0.20
validation: The new run created new result directories and processed a later instruction normally.

when: Camera frame arrived after first instruction
what: Some certificates showed `camera_seen=false` or camera age null.
why: The controller had not yet received a camera frame before the instruction was processed.
how_fixed: I waited for the first-camera-frame log before sending the evidence instruction.
commands:
timeout 10s ros2 topic hz /camera/color/image_raw
make vln-send TEXT="move forward slowly..."
validation: Later certificates showed camera_seen=true and frame_id=camera_color_optical_frame.

when: Camera rate is low for future live motion
what: Camera rate was about 1 Hz.
why: For dry-run evidence this proves vision connectivity, but for fast live motion it may be too slow depending on control speed and environment.
how_fixed: I bounded the current claim to dry-run evidence and listed camera-rate stabilization as a live-motion prerequisite.
commands: timeout 10s ros2 topic hz /camera/color/image_raw
validation: The rate was measured and documented rather than ignored.

when: Local modifications present while committing evidence
what: `git status --short` showed modified Makefile, audit logs, and untracked results.
why: Debugging creates local changes and many timestamped run outputs.
how_fixed: I committed only the curated demo evidence folder and left incidental logs out of the evidence commit.
commands:
git add results/demo_evidence_voice_vln_real_robot/
git commit -m "evidence: add voice-conditioned real-robot VLN safety trace"
validation: Commit 8bef749 contained exactly five evidence files.

what: A JSON field such as `"camera_seen": false` was pasted as a shell command and caused `command not found`.
why: Human-in-the-loop debugging with many terminals makes copy-paste errors easy.
how_fixed: I separated command blocks from output blocks in the reproducible manuscript and evidence package.
commands: python3 -m json.tool results/demo_evidence_voice_vln_real_robot/latest_voice_certificate_pretty.json
validation: Pretty JSON files prevent raw output from being confused with commands.
